title: Performance and cleanup: GIL held during solver ops, lock churn, dead version guards
author: devdanzin
body:
Three low-priority improvement opportunities:

### 1. GIL held during all solver operations

All solver methods (`addConstraint`, `removeConstraint`, `addEditVariable`, `removeEditVariable`, `suggestValue`, `updateVariables`, `reset`) call into the kiwi C++ library without releasing the GIL. For large constraint systems, this blocks all other Python threads during solving.

Releasing the GIL requires restructuring the try/catch blocks to catch exceptions inside the GIL-released region and convert to error codes, then set Python exceptions after reacquiring:

```cpp
int err = 0;
Py_BEGIN_ALLOW_THREADS
ACQUIRE_GLOBAL_LOCK();
try { self->solver.addConstraint(cn->constraint); }
catch (const kiwi::DuplicateConstraint&) { err = 1; }
catch (const kiwi::UnsatisfiableConstraint&) { err = 2; }
RELEASE_GLOBAL_LOCK();
Py_END_ALLOW_THREADS
if (err == 1) { PyErr_SetObject(DuplicateConstraint, other); return 0; }
```

This is a larger change and only matters for applications solving large constraint systems from multiple threads.

### 2. Lock churn in convert_to_kiwi_expression

`util.h:178-198` acquires and releases `global_lock` once per term in the expression (N+1 lock/unlock cycles for N terms). Since `kiwi::Term` construction is trivially cheap, the lock overhead dominates. Holding the lock for the entire conversion loop would be more efficient.

### 3. Dead version guards in 4 traverse functions

`variable.cpp:75-78`, `term.cpp:59-62`, `constraint.cpp:64-67`, `expression.cpp:65-68` all have:

```cpp
#if PY_VERSION_HEX >= 0x03090000
    Py_VISIT(Py_TYPE(self));
#endif
```

Since the minimum version is 3.10, the guard is always true and can be removed.

Found by [cext-review-toolkit](https://github.com/devdanzin/cext-review-toolkit).

comments.author: MatthieuDartiailh
comments.body: The first point is a conscious decision since kiwisolver is not threadsafe (use Rc like smart pointer for performance reason) and hence holding the GIL/critical_section is required for safety reasons.
